channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.
channel.copyright: Copyright:(C) Wed, 01 Jul 2026 05:34:12 IST Bennett Coleman & Co. Ltd, http://in.indiatimes.com/policyterms/1554651.cms

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: ‘Microphone theory’: Candace Owens revives controversial claim in Charlie Kirk assassination case
item.description: Charlie Kirk was killed during a TPUSA event in Utah in September 2025, and prosecutors accuse Tyler James Robinson of carrying out a politically motivated assassination. While investigators say forensic evidence strongly supports the lone-gunman case, Candace Owens has continued raising doubts and promoting the “exploding microphone” theory. Her claims have fueled debate online as Robinson’s high-profile case heads toward trial.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Who is Katalina Cartmel? Meet Jack Doherty’s girlfriend after explosive Ring camera footage goes viral
item.description: A Ring camera video shared by TMZ shows YouTuber Jack Doherty and girlfriend Katalina Cartmel involved in a heated argument. Cartmel appears to throw a water bottle at Doherty before leaving the room. The clip has sparked strong reactions online. Cartmel is a social media influencer with a large TikTok following, while Doherty remains one of YouTube’s most controversial creators.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Don’t broadcast Sheikh Hasina speeches: Bangladesh warns its media outlets
item.dc:creator: Ahsan Tasnim

item.title: 'Ready for war': Iran draws red line for US, says no final talks until MoU terms are met
item.description: Iran's Parliament Speaker has issued a stark warning of war if the US fails to adhere to a memorandum of understanding, emphasizing that negotiations will halt until Washington fulfills its commitments. Tehran views recent Persian Gulf incidents as violations, vowing a strong reaction. Discussions are currently focused on implementing the MoU, including the lifting of a naval blockade and the release of frozen assets, with technical talks planned in Doha.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Why Valkyrae believes Nina Lin should never return to Twitch after recent controversies
item.description: Popular YouTuber Valkyrae has voiced strong disapproval over streamer Nina Lin's return to Twitch, citing a pattern of serious controversies. Valkyrae highlighted past allegations involving creators DisguisedToast and Said, alongside Nina's recent arrest in New York for disorderly conduct. She argued these repeated incidents warrant a permanent ban, not a short suspension, questioning Twitch's consistency in enforcing its rules for creators facing multiple serious accusations.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: xQc survives scary Lamborghini scare after losing control during live track session
item.description: Popular streamer xQc narrowly avoided a serious crash while live-streaming from a Canadian racetrack. His high-performance Lamborghini Huracán Tecnica slid dangerously close to a trackside structure after hitting a curb. Thanks to xQc's quick reflexes and the instructor's intervention, the supercar was brought back under control, preventing a potential accident. The incident, captured on camera, highlighted the thrilling yet perilous nature of track driving.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: YourRAGE accuses childhood friend Greg of stealing thousands, ends 25-year friendship
item.description: Twitch streamer YourRAGE is heartbroken after accusing his childhood friend, Greg, of stealing a substantial sum, reportedly over $50,000. The streamer, who considered Greg like family and had supported him for 25 years, shared an emotional video detailing his sense of betrayal. This alleged theft has ended their decades-long friendship, with Greg yet to respond to the accusations.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Chinese proverb of the day: 'The toad wants to eat the swan' delivers a blunt reality check on overreaching ambition
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: ExtraEmily banned on Twitch after a near crash changes her latest livestream
item.description: ExtraEmily has been banned from Twitch after a June 28 IRL stream showed her nearly crashing while reading chat during a drive. The incident revived concerns about distracted driving and followed previous controversies, including a viewbotting dispute and an earlier driving-related suspension. The latest ban has again sparked debate over safety rules for livestreamers and the responsibility creators have while broadcasting in public.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Immigration attorney provides new update on Texas driver's license row, says it's a trap for H-1B, H-4, F1 visa holders
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Norwegian proverb of the day: "Haste is good...when the house is on fire" teaches us to take a pause, evaluate urgency
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: PIO Caribbean writer accused of using AI wins Commonwealth short story prize
item.dc:creator: Naomi Canton

item.title: Suppose the child hates US: Justice Alito's dissent as Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, whoever is born in US is a citizen
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'No direct US-Iran talks': Top US envoys reach Doha for Qatar-mediated negotiations
item.description: In Doha, US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner engaged with Qatari mediators to discuss Iran negotiations. They learned from both Qatar and Iran that no direct meetings would take place between US and Iranian officials, contradicting President Trump's earlier assertions. Instead, the conversations, organized by Qatar, will prioritize implementing a memorandum of understanding, which includes addressing the release of Iran's frozen assets.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: New visa rules from July 1 in US, Australia, Japan, Vietnam: What travelers, foreign workers must know
item.description: New visa rules July 1: The US is now offering a $750 fast-track tourist visa, and Australia is increasing the minimum salary for skilled migrants. Japan is hiking visa fees for numerous countries, Vietnam will require travelers to complete health declarations.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Harsh and toxic': US citizen shares harrowing experience with international school in Bengaluru, warns parents before moving back to India
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by Queen Elizabeth: "I declare before you all that my whole life whether it be long or short shall be devoted to…"
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Quote of the day by American social activist Alice Walker: "The most common way people give up their power is by…"
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Hands off' Strait of Hormuz: Iran says foreign intervention will 'only complicate issues'
item.description: Iran has confidently declared its readiness to maintain order in the Strait of Hormuz, insisting that foreign intervention is unnecessary and could destabilize the region. Furthermore, discussions with the US, arranged by Qatar, are expected to focus on the existing interim agreement rather than on new negotiations for a final settlement. Ongoing dialogue with Washington occurs through established political channels.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: UAE's first passenger train service launched as Etihad Rail begins operations
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
